svg.text: Multi-Layer Page Caching — CDN to Fragment Cache
CDN edge cache --> Reverse proxy (Varnish) --> Application cache --> Fragment cache, with TTL and hit rates
On MISS: forward to app server
On MISS: render page, cache, return
Cumulative Hit Rate Waterfall — How Each Layer Catches Requests
Static pages, assets, ESI templates -- TTL 5min-24hr
Origin load reduced from 10,000 req/s to 1,260 req/s (87.4% reduction)
Locations: 200+ PoPs globally. Serves closest PoP.
VCL rules: bypass cache for auth users, cart pages.
Grace mode: serve stale while fetching fresh backend.
Redis for distributed, Caffeine for in-process (L1).
Cache personalized HTML fragments per user session.
Assembly: merge fragments into final HTML in ~2ms.
Invalidation Flow — Purging Across All Layers
Content Update: Product price changed from $99 to $79 (triggering invalidation)
Purge by URL pattern or tags
Ban by URL or header pattern
Full invalidation complete in ~2 seconds across all layers
During this window, some layers may serve stale data (eventual consistency)
- Stale window: During propagation, different layers serve different versions
- Tag-based purge: Use cache tags (Surrogate-Key) for bulk invalidation
- Soft purge: Mark stale, serve with stale-while-revalidate header
- Cascade purge: One content change can invalidate hundreds of cache keys
CDN purge: 50-200ms propagation (depends on PoP count and API latency)
Varnish purge: 10-50ms (local network, direct HTTP BAN)
Redis DEL: O(1) per key, or O(N) for pattern match (use SCAN, not KEYS)
Caffeine invalidation: O(1) per entry, or batch invalidate via cache tags
